name: Feature DoR — Data & Reporting Extensions
description: Two additional criteria for Data & Reporting features — data contract and lineage map. Layered on top of the base Feature DoR.

criteria.name: Data Contract Defined
criteria.description: Output schema (columns, types, semantics) is documented as a contract that downstream consumers can build against.
criteria.why: Producers and consumers iterating on a verbal schema agreement leads to integration breakage.
criteria.fix: Document the output schema in a shared location (Confluence, Git, schema registry); name a schema owner.
criteria.good_example: Schema: dw_mart.delinquency_daily (loan_id BIGINT NOT NULL, reporting_date DATE NOT NULL, delinquency_pct DECIMAL(5,2)). Owner: Risk Reporting team. Versioned in git at schemas/delinquency_daily.yml.

criteria.name: End-to-End Lineage Map
criteria.description: Lineage from upstream source through every transformation layer to the consumer is mapped — at the feature level — and visible in the data catalog.
criteria.why: Without an end-to-end lineage, impact analysis when an upstream feed changes becomes guesswork.
criteria.fix: Trace the lineage in the data catalog (e.g. Collibra, OpenLineage). Confirm upstream owners and downstream consumers are tagged.
criteria.good_example: Upstream: EDW.loan_performance (owner: Acquisitions team) → ETL job mart_delinquency_daily.py → dw_mart.delinquency_daily → Risk Reporting Dashboard (consumer: Risk Committee). Cataloged in OpenLineage on 2026-04-30.
